paper_title: Independence number in triangle-free graphs avoiding a minor
summary: The conjecture that every n-vertex K_{t+1}-minor-free graph has independence number at least n/t remains open. The source paper itself proves a weaker result restricted to triangle-free graphs (independence number at least n/t^{1-ε} for all sufficiently large t), while the best known general bound without the triangle-free restriction is the Duchet–Meyniel bound α(G) ≥ n/(2t−1) from 1982. Recent breakthroughs on Hadwiger-type coloring (Norin–Postle–Song, Delcourt–Postle giving O(t log log t)-colorability) do not suffice to resolve this independence number conjecture, and no paper proving or disproving the full statement was found.

internal_refs_verified.note: Paper by Steiner concerns hypergraph coloring with excluded minors (bounding hypergraph chromatic number for K_t-minor-free hypergraphs); it does not address the independence number conjecture of Dvořák–Yepremyan.

notes: The conjecture is a well-known weakening of Hadwiger's conjecture: Hadwiger would imply chromatic number ≤ t for K_{t+1}-minor-free graphs and thus α(G) ≥ n/t, but the conjecture may be provable independently. The best unconditional general bound remains Duchet–Meyniel (α ≥ n/(2t−1)). A related paper arXiv:2002.11100 extends the Dvořák–Yepremyan triangle-free result to arbitrary H-free graphs (clique minors given independence number), but this is a different direction and does not resolve Conjecture 1. No follow-up paper resolving the full conjecture was found in the indexed literature.
